Independent read-only review (Read/Grep/Glob) supports closeout.

production_ready claim: The pack honestly holds production_ready=false and runtime_ready=false across manifest, construction-inspection.json (verdict PENDING, pack_production_ready=false), command-evidence.json, and adjudication.md. No inflated production/runtime/enterprise-trust claim exists; production_ready_flag is a named descriptor-verification token, not a boolean true. The pack is correctly pending_review with empty valid/invalid review receipts, and this hardened review is the gating review.

Implementation: CP719->CP720->CP721 binding chain is consistent across registry.js:443, contract current_pack/latest_pack (CP00-720, upstream CP00-719, next CP00-721 / RP23.P09.M06.S09), README #cp00-720, and the manifest plan_binding_snapshot. CP720_REQUIREMENTS distribution matches the manifest exactly (deliverables hermes_evidence:1, implementation:3, claude_review:2, security_audit:2, test:1, ui:1; micro titles Permission And Audit Binding:2, Synthetic Fixture Set:8; phase RP23.P09:10; M05:2/M06:8). Descriptor, case-set, coverage, and validator factories follow the established CP694-719 pattern and chain from the CP719 descriptor; index.js re-exports registry/service/validators; the contract regenerates deterministically (validate-rp23-external-integrations-ii-contract.mjs) with validation.valid=true and CP720 coverage+descriptor errors=[].

Boundaries: A comprehensive NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (descriptor_only, no_real_data, synthetic_only, writes_product_state=false, no bank/card/WEHAGO/Douzone/tax/DART runtime, no permission-decision/audit-body/Hermes-packet-body exposure, customer_safe_errors_only) is applied via freezeResult and embedded in the contract projection. validateDescriptor enforces claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, descriptor-only rows, no sensitive payloads, and runtime-closed integration gates. model.test.js covers CP00-720 binding/coverage, runtime-closed boundaries (runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-721, hermes emits_runtime_receipt=false, claude read_only=true), and contract latest-projection while CP694-719 stay historical. Independent grep for credentials/secrets/PII across packages/finance-integrations returned no matches.

Hermes: control-plane patterns are used only for deterministic evidence packet/receipt handling (receipt_shape descriptor_only_finance_integrations_no_write, emits_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false), not product runtime execution.

Review hardening (CP178+): readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, stdout_bytes 1436, well-formed JSON ok:true) - no auth failure or 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped evidence; validation-report shows prompt 2470B within 25600B limit, prompt excludes full diff/source, source_inspection_mode read_tools_used, summary_only correctly prohibited (security/audit + review-evidence deliverables present), review schema present, no forbidden tools. command-evidence records all required validations (node --check, npm test, npm run validate/closeout-pack-plan:validate/closeout-pack:validate/spec:requirements:validate/weighted:validate/fullplan:validate/goal:closeout:validate/rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check, rp23 contract validate) at exit_code 0.

Authority: Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not final approval authority; human/enterprise-trust approval is closed outside this receipt. Per instructions, blocks_goal_closeout is not set true merely due to that boundary, and the pack makes no runtime/enterprise-trust claim.

Benign observation (non-blocking): in changed-file-scope.json, docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md is listed under both untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. This is consistent (an untracked doc not part of CP720 active scope) and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so review completeness is unaffected. Not raised as a finding.

Result: PASS.
